title: Rossmanith Conjecture on Planar Induced Subgraphs
statement_text: An upper bound of the form $f(m) \leq \frac{1}{6}m + o(m)$ holds for the class of planar graphs, where $f(m)$ is the least integer such that every $m$-edge graph $G$ has a set $X$ of at most $f(m)$ vertices with $G - X$ planar.
context_text: Borradaile, Eppstein, and Zhu [1] proved $f(m) \leq \frac{23}{120}m$ for induced planar subgraphs and showed that if the Rossmanith bound were true it would be best possible, since for any proper minor-closed class $f(m) \geq \frac{1}{6}m - o(m)$. The conjecture is mentioned as an open problem in the graph-drawing context; the best known upper bound for arbitrary planar graphs remains $\frac{23}{120}m$.
paper_contribution: The paper does not make progress on this conjecture; it is cited in passing as an open problem while surveying related results on induced subgraphs in minor-closed classes.
notes: Attributed to Rossmanith via [1] (Borradaile, Eppstein, Zhu). The paper's main result concerns K4-minor-free (treewidth ≤ 2) induced subgraphs of connected graphs, not planar subgraphs. PDF source — math notation may be garbled.
paper_title: $K_{4}$-Minor-Free Induced Subgraphs of Sparse Connected Graphs
